mappings.rationale: Directly covers nutritional patterns, essential nutrients, and the nutritional needs of organisms as outlined in this section.
Addresses the procurement and processing of nutrients, food materials, and nutritional requirements.
Covers photosynthesis as the production of food in green plants, and autotrophic nutritional patterns.
Addresses the human digestive system including the alimentary canal, enzyme secretions, and the biodynamics of digestion.
Covers the diversity of digestive systems across invertebrates and vertebrates, from vacuolar to tubular systems.
Covers the anatomical structures of the vertebrate digestive system including oral cavity, esophagus, stomach, intestines, pancreas, and liver.
Covers digestive enzymes and their role in the breakdown and processing of nutrients in alimentary systems.
Directly addresses teeth as part of the oral cavity in vertebrate digestive systems, relevant to methods of ingestion.
Covers syntrophism and nutritional interrelationships where metabolic products of one organism provide essential nutrients for another.
